records.verificationNote: Read directly from the Illinois Medicaid Preferred Drug List, 'Effective July 1, 2026' (stated on page 1 and in the page footer of every page). The Bone Density Regulators drug-class table on page 70 of 150 lists 'ALENDRONATE SODIUM' in both SOLN and TABS dosage forms with status PREFERRED — the only Preferred entries in the entire osteoporosis-drug portion of this class. All other oral bisphosphonate entries on the same page (Fosamax, Binosto, Fosamax Plus D, generic ibandronate sodium, Actonel, generic risedronate sodium, Atelvia, risedronate sodium DR) are explicitly labeled NON_PREFERRED in the same table. Per Illinois HFS policy (confirmed via web search of HFS/regulations.justia material, since this PDL document itself carries no narrative PA-criteria text for this class beyond the page-1 legend, which does not call out bone density regulators specifically), Non-Preferred PDL status is enforced through the prior-approval process — i.e., Non-Preferred = PA required — so the pa/nonformulary tags above are a confident, standard-convention inference from the PREFERRED/NON_PREFERRED status codes actually printed in the document, not verbatim PA-criteria text (no class specifically calls out oral bisphosphonates in the page-1 legend bullets). No brand-name ibandronate (Boniva) entry appears anywhere in the 150-page document at all — flagged as nonformulary rather than omitted.

This inference was confirmed generically (that J3489/zoledronic acid is billed as a medical-benefit infusion, standard across payers) via a web search, but no IL HFS-specific medical-benefit or physician-administered-drug policy document was independently fetched to confirm IL Medicaid's exact PA posture for J3489 zoledronic acid — so the preferred-agent identity (zoledronic acid/Reclast, the only IV bisphosphonate class member) is asserted from the class definition itself, and the absence of a pharmacy-benefit restriction is inferred from the billing-pathway explanation above, not read verbatim off an IL HFS document. Mark as partial rather than verified for this reason; a follow-up fetch of IL HFS's physician-administered-drug fee schedule or medical PA policy would be needed to upgrade this to verified.

records.verificationNote: Read directly from the 'OSTEOPOROSIS THERAPY' subsection, PDF page 135 (printed page 121) of the Wellcare Value Script (PDP) 2026 Formulary, updated 07/01/2026. Confirmed verbatim: 'raloxifene oral tablet 60 mg 2' -- Tier 2, with no PA, QL, or step-therapy flag listed alongside it (unlike most other osteoporosis-class entries which do carry a QL). Raloxifene is the only SERM found in the document; brand Evista does not appear anywhere in the full-text search of the 190-page document, consistent with a fully genericized single-source drug where the brand isn't separately formulary-listed (normal automatic generic substitution, not a coverage barrier, so not flagged as non-formulary). The once-daily sig is FDA-label standard-of-care, not stated verbatim in the drug-list document itself.
